title: Add a new task

story.i_want_to: add a new task by typing a command with a description
story.so_that: I can capture things I need to do without leaving the terminal

rationale: Adding tasks is the foundational action of a todo app. Without it, no other features are useful. The add command must be simple and provide clear feedback so users know their task was saved.

acceptance_criteria.description: Running `todo add "Buy groceries"` creates a new task with description 'Buy groceries'.
The new task is assigned a unique integer ID that is one greater than the highest existing ID (or 1 if no tasks exist). IDs are never reused even after deletion.
The new task has a created_at field set to the current ISO 8601 timestamp.
The new task has completed_at set to null.
The task is persisted to ~/.todo/tasks.json immediately using the atomic write pattern.
The app prints a confirmation message to stdout: 'Added task {id}: {description}'.
Running `todo add` without a description prints 'Error: Please provide a task description.' to stderr and exits with code 1.

Running `todo add` with a whitespace-only description prints 'Error: Task description cannot be empty.' to stderr and exits with code 1. Descriptions are stripped of leading/trailing whitespace before saving.
Task descriptions containing embedded newlines are flattened to single-line (newlines replaced with spaces).

test_cases.name: Add first task to empty list
expected_results: Returns task with id=1, description='Buy groceries', status='pending'
created_at is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp

test_cases.name: Add task increments ID from existing tasks
preconditions: tmp_dir with tasks.json containing one task with id=5

test_cases.name: Add task with empty description is rejected
expected_results: No task is added to tasks.json

test_cases.name: CLI add command end-to-end
steps: Run `python todo.py add 'Buy groceries'` with HOME=tmp_dir

test_cases.name: Whitespace-only description is rejected
expected_results: stderr contains 'Error: Task description cannot be empty.'

test_cases.name: Description with quotes and special characters is saved correctly
expected_results: Task is saved with description intact including quotes and ampersand

test_cases.name: Description with newlines is flattened
expected_results: Task description is 'Line one Line two' (newlines replaced with spaces)

technical_notes: Use sys.argv for argument parsing (keep it simple, no argparse yet)
ID assignment: max(task['id'] for task in tasks) + 1, default to 1
All success output to stdout, all errors to stderr
Strip whitespace and replace newlines before saving descriptions
Use ' '.join(sys.argv[2:]) to support unquoted multi-word descriptions
